taxonomy: Camaridium strumatum, also known as Dracula strumata, is a species of orchid. It is classified within the genus Dracula, which is a group of epiphytic orchids native to Central and South America. The genus Dracula is itself part of the subtribe Draculae, within the tribe Masdevallieae. The Masdevallieae tribe is a subtribe of the subfamily Orchidoideae, which includes many species of terrestrial and epiphytic orchids. The Orchidoideae subfamily is part of the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family.
